findings.evidence: `groupLikeTrivialMap` is defined generally at lines 155-168, while the id/composition lemmas are only `trivialMap_id`/`trivialMap_comp` at lines 293-307.
findings.fix: Prove `groupLikeTrivialMap_id` and `groupLikeTrivialMap_comp` for arbitrary `g : GroupLike R C`, then derive or delete the bialgebra-unit-only `trivialMap_id`/`trivialMap_comp` special cases.
findings.issue: `trivialMap_id` and `trivialMap_comp` are special cases of the already-defined `groupLikeTrivialMap`, leaving users of `GroupLikeTrivial R C M g` without the same id/composition simp API.

summary: The core construction is now at the coalgebra/group-like level, but the functorial API is still only proved for the bialgebra-unit specialization.
